_about: English catalog of user-facing strings for the contribution flow and its adjacent surfaces (landing, governance, submission status). Keys are dotted and semantic (surface.component.role). Values are plain English; no markdown. Prep work for localisation — the runtime reads this via /i18n.js and hydrates DOM via data-i18n attributes. No translation keys are active yet; English is the source of truth.

landing.title: Bridgn — Text and speech to sign language
landing.meta_description: Bridgn translates typed text, recorded speech, and uploaded video into sign-language animations. Free and open-source.
landing.og_title: Bridgn — Text and speech to sign language
landing.og_description: Translate typed text, recorded speech, and uploaded video into sign-language animations.
landing.hero_heading: Text and speech to sign language.
landing.hero_body: Bridgn translates typed text, recorded speech, and uploaded video into sign-language animations — entirely in your browser, free and open-source.
landing.credits_lead: Bridgn builds on work released by Deaf research institutions, linguists, and open-source contributors. Full attributions and licence terms are in the {{readme_link}}.
landing.credits_cwasa: CWASA — 3D avatar engine, Virtual Humans Group, University of East Anglia.
landing.credits_hamnosys: HamNoSys — notation system, Institute of German Sign Language, Universität Hamburg.
landing.credits_databases: Sign-language databases: DictaSign (BSL, GSL/LSF), SignAvatars (DGS, PJM), SignLanguageSynthesis (NGT), algerianSignLanguage-avatar, bdsl-3d-animation (Bangla), Text-to-Sign-Language and text_to_isl (Indian), KurdishSignLanguage, VSL (Vietnamese), syntheticfsl and signtyper (Filipino). See the README for each author and licence.

contribute.meta_description: Contribute a single sign to the Bridgn dictionary. Pick a sign language, describe the sign, review the notation, submit for Deaf review.
contribute.heading: Contribute a sign

language_picker.prompt: Which sign language are you contributing to?
language_picker.load_error: Could not load the language list. Refresh to retry.

reviewer_notice.no_reviewers_body: No Deaf reviewers are currently assigned to {{language}}. You can save a draft, but it will not be reviewed until a reviewer is added. We will email you if one becomes available.

token_prompt.heading: Resume this draft
token_prompt.body: To resume this draft, paste the resume token shown when you first created it.
token_prompt.label: Resume token
token_prompt.error_mismatch: That token does not match this session.

authoring.gloss_label: Gloss — the English word or short phrase for this sign.
authoring.gloss_error_required: Gloss is required before you describe the sign.
authoring.description_label: Describe the sign in plain language.
authoring.description_placeholder_generic: e.g. describe the handshape, where the hand starts, how it moves, and which way the palm faces.
authoring.description_error_too_short: Please add at least 20 characters of description.
authoring.deaf_native_checkbox: I am a Deaf native signer of this language.
authoring.submit_error_generic: Could not start a session. Please try again.
authoring.submit_error_language_unsupported: This language is not yet enabled on the server. Please pick another.
authoring.submit_error_rate_limited: You're sending requests faster than the server can process. Wait a moment and try again.
authoring.submit_error_injection_rejected: We didn't interpret this as a sign description. Please describe only the sign itself.

chat.correction_hint: Click on the avatar to target a specific moment or body part (optional).
chat.reply_user_question: I can only ask about this sign. If you'd like to learn HamNoSys, see the docs link below.
chat.generating_msg: Enough information to draft the sign. Preparing preview.
chat.ready_msg: Draft is ready. Review the preview below and either submit or describe a correction.
chat.error_msg: Something went wrong generating a clarification. Try rephrasing your description, or submit the draft as-is and let the reviewer fill gaps.
chat.error_rate_limited: You're sending requests faster than the server can process. Wait a moment and try again.
chat.error_injection_rejected: We didn't interpret this as a sign description. Please describe only the sign itself.

preview.fallback: Preview unavailable in this environment. The sign can still be submitted; the HamNoSys below is what the reviewer will evaluate.

notation.validation_fallback: The generated notation could not be validated. A reviewer will finish it.
notation.sigml_summary_suffix: element with the HamNoSys above, in the Kozha canonical format. Your renderer will accept it as-is.

submission.heading: Submit for review
submission.submit_error_no_session: This draft does not have an active session. Refresh and try again.
submission.submit_error_not_ready: The generated sign needs to be ready before you can submit.
submission.edit_modal_body: This will discard your draft for {{gloss_label}} so you can edit the gloss and description. Continue?
submission.change_lang_modal_body: This will discard your draft for {{gloss_label}}. Continue?

confirmation.body_pending: Your draft is now in the review queue for {{language}}. The typical review time is 3 days. You can return to this URL any time to check status.
confirmation.body_draft: Your draft is saved. {{language}} does not yet have Deaf reviewers assigned, so review is on hold. If a reviewer is added, we will update the status here. You can return to this URL any time.

status.meta_description: View the status of a sign you submitted to the Bridgn dictionary.
status.heading: Submission status
status.resume_body: To see the description you submitted and any reviewer notes, paste the resume token that was shown when you first created this draft.

note.draft: No Deaf reviewer is currently assigned to this language. Review is on hold.
note.under_review: A reviewer has this draft open.
note.validated: A Deaf reviewer has approved this sign.
note.rejected: A Deaf reviewer declined to publish this sign.
note.quarantined: This sign is on hold pending further review.

error.body_not_found: No submission matches this URL. It may have been discarded, or the link may be mistyped.
error.body_access_denied: The resume token did not match this submission.
error.body_network: Could not reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
error.body_invalid: This URL does not contain a submission id.
error.token_mismatch: That token does not match this submission.
error.token_network: Could not reach the server. Check your connection and try again.

governance.meta_description: How contributions to Bridgn are reviewed: who reviews, the Deaf advisory board, what happens to your submission, evaluation criteria, and how to raise a concern.
governance.heading: Governance
governance.lede: This page describes who reviews the signs you submit, what happens to them, and how to raise a concern about a sign already published. It is short on purpose. Everything below is a factual description of how review works — or, where something is not yet in place, a plain statement of that.
governance.reviewers_body_1: Every submission is reviewed by Deaf signers of the language it was submitted in. A sign is validated only after two reviewers of that language approve it. For each sign language Bridgn supports, at least one of the two reviewers must be a Deaf native signer of that language. Non-native reviewers may second an approval, but they cannot approve alone, and any non-native approval requires written justification that the Deaf native reviewer can later override.
governance.reviewers_body_2: Reviewers are onboarded individually and listed here by name, affiliation, and the language(s) they are accredited to review. If a language does not yet have Deaf native-signer reviewers, submissions in that language are saved as drafts and do not enter the review queue until reviewers are seated. We do not route signs to reviewers who do not sign the language natively.
governance.reviewers_empty: No reviewers are onboarded yet. Submissions are being accepted and saved as drafts, but the review queue is paused until the Deaf advisory board seats the first reviewers.
governance.reviewers_load_failure: Reviewer roster unavailable right now. If this persists, email the address at the bottom of this page.
governance.language_no_native_singular: {{total}} reviewer, none Deaf native — review paused
governance.language_no_native_plural: {{total}} reviewers, none Deaf native — review paused
governance.board_body: The Deaf advisory board is the project's accountability body. It approves reviewer appointments, arbitrates disputes between reviewers, and holds the authority to remove a sign from the public library without further discussion.
governance.board_empty: We are in the process of seating a Deaf advisory board. Until then, no signs are being exported to the Kozha library. Drafts continue to be accepted and queued; they will be reviewed once the board is seated and has approved the first reviewers.
governance.board_intro_populated: The following Deaf advisors have consented to be listed publicly as members of the board:
governance.flow_step_2: A Deaf reviewer of your chosen language evaluates the draft against the criteria below.
governance.flow_step_3: If approved by two native-signer reviewers, the sign enters the Kozha public library and becomes available in real-time translation.
governance.flow_step_4: If rejected, you receive the reviewer's feedback and can submit a revised version.
governance.criteria_accuracy_dd: The sign, as generated from your description, matches the meaning you intended to convey.
governance.criteria_cultural_dd: The sign does not carry unintended or offensive connotations in the Deaf community of the language it belongs to.
governance.criteria_regional_dd: The sign is recognisable to signers of the regional variant it is tagged with, and is not silently conflated with a different region's sign.
governance.criteria_phonological_dd: Handshape, orientation, location, and movement are formed in ways the language actually uses, not invented combinations.
governance.criteria_non_manual_dd: Required facial expression, mouthing, and body posture are present and correct — these carry linguistic meaning and are not decoration.
governance.criteria_reusability_dd: The sign is rendered in a form that will read clearly when combined with other signs in running translation, not only in isolation.
governance.concern_body: If you are a member of the Deaf community and believe a sign published on Kozha is inaccurate or inappropriate, email the address below. The sign will be quarantined within 24 hours pending review. This applies to anyone in the Deaf community — you do not need to be a registered reviewer to flag a sign. Any Deaf community member can refuse a published sign by email, and we treat that refusal as a quarantine trigger, not a request to be weighed against other voices.
governance.concern_email_note: We do not run a form, a ticketing portal, or a community-portal workflow for these concerns. A single email reaches the board; the board is the only group that releases a quarantined sign.
